codeReview.summary: The feature adds the expected deviation documentation sections for SW/WASM freshness and source-side sponsor-screen parity, and ties them to assertion IDs. However, the new sponsor-flow documentation contains an incorrect live route path, which makes the implementation pointer inaccurate in a docs-only feature whose deliverable is documentation correctness/completeness.

issues.description: The new deviation entry says the hand-off screen is at `/onboard-sponsor/package`, but the app routes and screen navigation use `/onboard-sponsor/handoff` (`src/app/CoreRoutes.tsx:59`, `src/screens/OnboardSponsorScreens.tsx:219`). This leaves the documentation incorrect for the exact flow it is meant to document.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The assigned skill (`ui-screen-worker`) is a poor procedural fit for pure documentation features, causing repeated required-procedure deviations even when the work is correctly scoped to docs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` is false and explicitly cites skipped `agent-browser` and RED-first test steps for this docs-only change; `.factory/skills/ui-screen-worker/SKILL.md` mandates UI-building and UI-verification workflow.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission contract expectations for VAL-ONBOARD-017 currently require a Paper artboard and a `/demo/{scenario-id}` entry, but the code/docs baseline for source-side sponsor flow states no such artboard exists and no demo scenario is present; this mismatch can cause validator ambiguity.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `validation-contract.md:1548-1549` requires "A Paper artboard exists and is referenced in `src/demo/scenarios.ts`"; `docs/runtime-deviations-from-paper.md:1040-1041` states no sponsor demo scenario; `src/demo/scenarios.ts` has no `onboard-sponsor` scenario entries.

summary: Review result: FAIL. Commit 612a12fe adds the intended deviation sections, but includes a blocking route-path documentation error (`/onboard-sponsor/package` vs actual `/onboard-sponsor/handoff`). Shared-state observations were also recorded for skill fit and contract-vs-baseline parity expectations.
